title: erodium cicutarium
intro: Erodium cicutarium, also known as redstem filaree or redstem storksbill, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the geranium family, native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Erodium cicutarium has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, easing menstrual cramps, and treating digestive issues due to its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies for its expectorant properties to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis.
